method_atom: A method that maps the linearized fluctuations riding on a slowly varying background medium onto an effective wave equation in a curved effective geometry, so that a prescribed time-dependence of the medium's effective coupling parametrically amplifies fluctuation modes and creates pairs of excitation quanta. The fluctuating field is evolved numerically via a stochastic classical-field approximation (truncated Wigner) that seeds initial quantum fluctuations, and short-wavelength behaviour departs from the ideal long-wavelength result through modified dispersion relations inherited from the medium's microscopic structure.
object_atom: A two-dimensional homogeneous Bose-Einstein condensate of a weakly interacting Bose gas whose s-wave scattering length is varied in time, used as an analogue of cosmological particle creation in an expanding Friedmann-Robertson-Walker universe.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the effective-curved-geometry mapping with time-varying-background pair creation plus the truncated-Wigner classical-field simulation scheme; the domain-specific part is the weakly interacting Bose gas standing in for FRW cosmology.
